problem_text: Determine whether sum_{n=1}^infinity sin^2(n)/n^2 converges or diverges.

content.label: Positive-term series
content.applies_when: sin^2(n) >= 0, so the terms are nonnegative: this is a positive-term series, not an alternating one, and direct comparison applies

content.meaning: sum 1/n^p converges iff p > 1; the comparison series 1/n^2 has p = 2 and converges.

content.label: choose the comparison series sum 1/n^2, a convergent p-series (p=2)
content.action: choose the comparison series sum 1/n^2, a convergent p-series (p=2)
content.purpose: pick a known convergent series to bound the given one

content.label: establish 0 <= sin^2(n)/n^2 <= 1/n^2 for all n
content.purpose: show the given terms are dominated by the convergent series

content.label: Direct Comparison Test (convergence)
content.applies_when: a nonnegative series bounded above term-by-term by a convergent series also converges.

content.label: since sum 1/n^2 converges and the given terms are smaller, sum sin^2(n)/n^2 converges (absolutely) by Direct Comparison
content.action: since sum 1/n^2 converges and the given terms are smaller, sum sin^2(n)/n^2 converges (absolutely) by Direct Comparison
